introSection: Hurricane was a wooden hybrid roller coaster manufactured by Custom Coasters International, Inc. that was located at Myrtle Beach Pavilion in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina, USA. It operated from 2000 to 2006.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Hurricane officially opened on May 6, 2000.

On September 30, 2006, Myrtle Beach Pavilion closed to the public. Demolition of Hurricane began in early 2007. While Hurricane was scrapped, Kings Island bought the trains and used them for Son of Beast starting in the 2007 season.

fullText:
Hurricane was a wooden hybrid roller coaster manufactured by Custom Coasters International, Inc. that was located at Myrtle Beach Pavilion in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina, USA. It operated from 2000 to 2006.

Hurricane officially opened on May 6, 2000.

On September 30, 2006, Myrtle Beach Pavilion closed to the public. Demolition of Hurricane began in early 2007. While Hurricane was scrapped, Kings Island bought the trains and used them for Son of Beast starting in the 2007 season.
